entries.title: SMART Notebook lessons and a PLC redesign

items.text: Import your existing SMART Notebook lessons — pages keep their original order and embedded images render correctly.
Open any page to edit it: move, resize, duplicate, or retype objects, and add, delete, or reorder pages.
Write on slides live with pen, highlighter, and eraser tools.
Too big to import? A built-in converter shrinks large lessons right in your browser — no install needed.
A redesigned PLC space with side navigation and a clean Home dashboard.
Author quizzes and video activities and set up assignments without ever leaving the PLC.
A filterable view of shared student results, embedded Google Docs for shared notes, and one-click resource import into your PLC library.
Admins can push curated resources and assignments to specific PLCs or to all of them at once.
Your Style, Preferences, and Language options are now grouped into a single Settings modal instead of separate sidebar panels.
Music widget playback controls — play, skip, repeat, and shuffle — now look and work the same across every view, and the tab pills fill the bar and shrink to icons on narrow widgets.
PLC quiz and video-activity assignments sync reliably again after silently failing for some teachers.

details.text: SMART Notebook import — bring your existing SMART Notebook lessons into SpartBoard. Pages now arrive in their true display order (instead of being scrambled), and embedded images that previously showed up blank render faithfully.

Edit imported pages — open any page in the editor to select an object and move it, resize it from the corners, duplicate it, or double-click to retype its text. You can also add a blank page, delete a page, or reorder pages within a lesson, and every change is saved to the cloud.

Write on slides — a floating pen, highlighter, and eraser palette lets you annotate any imported page live, the way you would on a SMART board. Your ink becomes a normal object you can move, resize, or delete, and it sticks around when you save.

Built-in lesson converter — if a SMART Notebook file is too large to import directly, a styled prompt now opens a converter that shrinks it entirely in your browser (no download or install required) and hands back an optimized file ready to import. A real 14 MB, 230-page deck compresses to about 6 MB with no visible quality loss.

PLC collaborative space redesign — the PLC dashboard moves from a row of tabs to a clear side-rail navigation with a redesigned Home page. Members and quick-create actions now live in the page header, and the dashboard fills the full width of the screen.
Author and assign inside the PLC — create quizzes and video activities and fully configure their assignments right within the PLC, without first building them on a board.

Shared data, notes, and resources — browse shared student results in a filterable view (by type, assignment, teacher, class, or date), keep shared notes in an embedded Google Doc, and pull a resource into your PLC library or jump to its assignment with a single click.
Admin-curated PLC resources — admins can push selected resources and assignments to specific PLCs or to every PLC at once, making it easy to roll shared materials out across teams.

Unified Settings modal — your Style, Preferences, and Language & Region options now live together in one focused Settings modal with its own Appearance, Dock, Behavior, and Language sections, instead of being split across separate sidebar panels. Dock position and dock styling sit side by side, and clear "This board" / "All boards" labels show whether a setting applies everywhere or just to the current board.

Music widget controls — play, pause, skip, repeat, and shuffle now behave consistently across the compact bar, minimal view, and full player. The tab pills span the full width of the bar and collapse to clean icons on narrow widgets instead of truncating their labels.
Admin Settings now uses the same side-rail navigation for moving between sections.

PLC assignment sync — quizzes and video activities created with PLC sync turned on now record correctly again. A recent change to how those assignments are stored had been silently blocking the sync for affected teachers.
Music widget repeat and shuffle now recover on their own if Spotify briefly loses track of the active device, instead of appearing stuck in the wrong state.

entries.title: Boards card actions and follow-up fixes

items.text: Share and Duplicate icons now sit directly on every Board and Collection card — no more digging into the right-click menu on tablets and projector touchscreens.
Duplicate a whole Collection at once — name, color, and every board inside come along (sub-collections are left in place).
Duplicate feels instant: the new card appears in the grid immediately, the icon shows a spinner while the save finishes, and rapid clicks no longer create extra copies.
The new Backgrounds modal now tracks built-in colors, patterns, and gradients in Recents (not just custom uploads).
If a background upload is rejected, picking the same file again now works instead of appearing stuck.
Record and Magic can be removed from Quick Access slots again after they were chosen.
Quiz and Video Activity now catch students clicking into the browser's URL bar or bookmark dropdown — recent browser changes had quietly let some focus-loss events slip past.
Text Widget bullet and numbered list toolbar buttons now apply formatting on a fresh single-line block (keyboard shortcuts were unaffected).

details.text: Boards manager — Share and Duplicate icons now sit alongside Pin on every Board and Collection card, so the most common actions are one tap away on tablets and projector touchscreens. The Share icon honors your existing dashboard-sharing permission setting, and the right-click menu still works exactly the same way.

Duplicate a Collection — copy a whole Collection (name + color) along with every board inside it in one action. Sub-collections are left in place so the operation stays bounded; you can re-run Duplicate on any sub-folder you also want to copy.

Duplicate (Boards and Collections) now feels instant — the new card appears in the grid immediately, the icon flips to a spinner while the save finishes in the background, and rapid taps no longer queue up multiple copies. If the save fails, the optimistic card is removed and the existing error toast still fires.

Quiz and Video Activity tab-switch detection now catches clicks into the browser's URL bar, bookmark dropdown, devtools, and other browser-chrome targets. Recent Chrome and Firefox changes had quietly stopped firing focus-loss events for those targets, so a few students could leave the tab without triggering the warning overlay. The published-results view is intentionally unchanged so a quick URL-bar click on a posted score doesn't lock students out.

Text Widget bullet-list and numbered-list toolbar buttons now apply the list formatting on a fresh single-line text block instead of just showing the button as active. Keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Shift+7 / Ctrl+Shift+8) were not affected.
Backgrounds modal — built-in colors, patterns, and gradients are now tracked in Recents alongside custom uploads, instead of only My Uploads showing up there.
Backgrounds upload — if a file is rejected (too large, Drive not ready, etc.), selecting the same file again now triggers a new upload attempt instead of silently doing nothing.
Quick Access Widgets modal — Record and Magic can now be removed from your slots once added. They were previously stranded in the selected list with no way to deselect.

items.text: Backgrounds — a new full modal with Favorites, Recents, dynamic Media categories, Colors, Patterns, Gradients, and My Uploads. Click the star on any background to favorite it.
Quick Access Widgets — new modal with a search bar, a 6-column grid, and an "X/2 slots" counter that updates as you pick.
Roster editor now shows PIN, first name, last name, and restrictions by default, and toggle colors match the rest of the app (colored = on, gray = off).
Sidebar Style panel applies every change live — the Save/Discard step is gone and sliders feel smoother.
Modals, popovers, banners, toasts, and menus across the app now fade and slide in as designed instead of popping in instantly.

details.text: Backgrounds picker redesigned — new full modal with a left rail of Favorites, Recents, Media categories, Colors, Patterns, Gradients, and My Uploads. Star any background to favorite it, and recently used backgrounds are tracked automatically. Custom colors, gradients, and Drive uploads all live in the same place.
Quick Access Widgets redesigned — new modal with sticky search, a 6-column grid, an "X/2 slots" counter, and live-apply when you click a tile.

What's New panel redesigned with a curated, themed overview by default and a "Read full update" link for the full patch notes. The date now formats in your language and the redundant version label is gone.
Admins can tag background presets directly from the Background Manager (suggestions are pulled from your existing tags) to keep large libraries organized and easier to find.
Admins can restrict any global feature to specific buildings in Global Settings — roll a feature out school by school instead of district-wide.

Roster (My Classes) editor — PIN, first name, last name, and restrictions are visible by default, and the toggle button colors flipped to match the rest of the app (colored = on, gray = off). Email stays off by default for privacy.
Sidebar Style panel — removed the Save/Discard footer; every control now applies live as you change it, with smoother sliders.
Animations restored — modals, popovers, banners, toasts, FAB menus, and sidebar tabs now fade and slide in as designed instead of mounting instantly.

entries.title: Collections, sharing, and a Boards manager refresh

items.text: Group your boards into folders so a busy dashboard list stays organized.
Pick a color, then drag boards into the Collection from the Boards manager.
Share a whole Collection with a colleague, or save it as a template to reuse next year.
Pin frequently used boards so they appear at the top regardless of which Collection is active.
Two new options when publishing quiz results for student review:
Watermark — a faint identifier behind each student's responses so screenshots are traceable.
Tab-navigation lock — pick how many times a student can leave the results tab before it locks.
Unlock one student at a time without reopening the whole assignment.
Every card shows a visual preview of its widgets, and you can drag from anywhere on the card.
Collections and Boards each get their own button instead of a shared kebab menu.

details.text: Collections — group your boards into folders, share a whole folder with another teacher, or save one as a template to reuse next year.
Pin boards across Collections — your favorites now appear at the top of the bottom-left Boards menu no matter which folder they live in.
Quiz results — watermark + warning if students try to screenshot, and you can now unlock one student at a time without reopening the whole assignment.
Boards manager refreshed — every card now shows a visual preview of the widgets, you can drag from anywhere on the card, and Collections/Boards each get their own button instead of a single kebab.
Quieter Google Drive reconnect prompt — one banner per session instead of one for every failed sync.
Quiz rapid-tap lockout now fires reliably on the first tap — answers no longer slip past after a fast double-tap.
Sound Widget no longer gets stuck silent after switching browser tabs.
Text editor selection now extends across paragraphs and list items instead of stopping at the first block.
Click the new "What's New" link on the Update Available toast — or open it any time from the bottom of the sidebar menu — to see a friendly summary of what changed before you refresh.
